title: Hardcoded mainnet HEX address can brick or mis-settle the market on the wrong chain
reason: This is a deployment/configuration hazard rather than an in-protocol exploit: the code is explicitly pinned to one token address, and harm arises only if operators deploy it on an unintended chain or fork.

title: Settlement trusts ERC20 return values instead of verifying exact token movement
reason: The contract is not token-agnostic; it hardcodes a single HEX address. The proposed short-transfer/malicious-token scenarios require either a non-standard token at that address or an already-misdeployed market, making the issue too speculative as a standalone finding.

title: ETH or HEX sent outside offer flows becomes permanently stranded
reason: This only affects unsolicited or accidental transfers outside the supported offer workflow, and does not create protocol-level insolvency, theft, or permissionless DoS within intended usage.
